policy: 2025–2026 proven production R&D + Unity 6 engine refs only. Speculative preprints excluded. Full catalog on disk; agent prompt uses rung-filtered subset. Florida aquifer/LiDAR: cave structure only.

visualReferences.title: Unity 6 Terrain — Heightmaps (carved mouth / surface walk-in)
visualReferences.notes: Surface entrance carve + alphamap paint; underground tube uses RouteTerrain meshes.

visualReferences.title: Unity 6 Terrain — Paint Texture (rock / dirt layers)
visualReferences.notes: Entrance path rock paint after height carve.

visualReferences.title: Unity 6 MeshData — procedural floor/ceiling strips
visualReferences.notes: RouteTerrainFloor / RouteTerrainCeiling mesh builders in kit.

visualReferences.title: Unity 6 NavMesh — baked walkable cave floor
visualReferences.notes: NavMesh on RouteTerrainFloor collider after shell pass.

visualReferences.title: Unity 6 Lighting — baked interior readability
visualReferences.notes: Materials + light probes for cave readability (materials rung).

visualReferences.title: Ubisoft — Far Cry 6 open world (Yara terrain + interiors)
visualReferences.notes: Reference for large-scale outdoor terrain, rock formations, and interior contrast. GDC22 raytracing + weather systems.

visualReferences.title: Ubisoft — Far Cry 5 procedural biome terrain (GDC)

visualReferences.title: Ubisoft La Forge — PCG / Electric Dreams (environment scale)

visualReferences.title: EA SEED — ML AAA game testing (automation reference)

visualReferences.title: Epic Games — Unreal Landscape (openings for caves)
visualReferences.notes: Epic docs explicitly mention terrain openings for caves. Cross-check Unity RouteTerrain approach.

visualReferences.title: Epic GPUOpen — procedural mesh generation
visualReferences.notes: Production mesh tooling for shell/wall density.

visualReferences.title: NVIDIA Research — 3D Generalist (world-scale meshes)
visualReferences.notes: High-end mesh/world reference — use for silhouette only; kit uses block rings not neural gen.

visualReferences.title: Sony — automated gameplay testing (PS5)
visualReferences.notes: Route/playtest automation parity with CavePlaytestRouteBot goals.

visualReferences.title: Activision Research — production QA scale
visualReferences.notes: QA/combat probe validation patterns.

visualReferences.title: Bungie — engineering / large worlds
visualReferences.notes: Performance + large world streaming reference.

visualReferences.title: Riot Games — performance optimization (Swarm)
visualReferences.notes: Triangle budget / perf discipline for grading pass.

visualReferences.title: Microsoft Research — gameplay simulation (production ML)
visualReferences.notes: Optional ideation only — prefer shipped environment art above for layout.

visualReferences.title: Reference — underground interior lighting (Elden Ring mine)
visualReferences.notes: Cave/mine readability + floor/ceiling contrast reference (shipped game art, not R&D paper).

visualReferences.title: Reference — interior rock corridors (DOOM Eternal)
visualReferences.notes: Tight corridor rock walls + combat arenas — shell ring layout reference.

visualReferences.title: Environment Kit — interstitial vegetation spread (dual grid pass)
visualReferences.notes: Kit implementation: pass-1 uniform grid per terrain tile, pass-2 half-cell offset grid (+38% spacing) skipping slots within min separation of pass-1 instances. Matches Guerrilla GPU scatter after terrain rules — scatter points separate from heightfield. Use SurfaceIntelligentPropPlacer.TryInterstitialWideSpreadPass after primary categories.

visualReferences.title: Unity 6 — Terrain trees & details (distance / density)
visualReferences.notes: Authoring analog: prefab scatter per tile with slope filter and min separation; do not paint only along splines. Tree distance and detail density inform per-tile TargetPerTile counts in SurfaceTerrainPropPlacementRegion.

visualReferences.title: Florida karst — surface entrance lip + structural void below (DS 926)
visualReferences.notes: Walkable surface at cave opening; tube/route in Floridan structural unit below land surface. Kit: CaveUndergroundEntranceEnforcer — align opening, FinalizeGroundPlacement, mouth depth snap, SurfaceWalkIn descent mesh + floor modules, trail connector, atmosphere trigger. Do not use water table or inundation DEM for void Y.

visualReferences.title: Sony Guerrilla — Horizon terrain sculpt (open world rock)
visualReferences.notes: Natural rock terrain and vista composition for surface mouth.

visualReferences.title: World generation pipeline ladder — ordered phases (synthesis)
visualReferences.studio: Environment Kit (curated from AAA + engine R&D)
visualReferences.notes: LADDER RULE: one global FIFO queue; each rung consumes immutable artifacts from upstream and writes new artifacts downstream. Never rerun a completed rung unless its inputs changed (seed, scope, or upstream mask). Invalidate downstream only. ORDER (Environment Kit mapping): (0) research+seed lock; (1) macro terrain + Florida hillshade reference; (2) hydrology/karst masks (structure only, no water sim); (3) trails/splines + NavMesh walk band; (4) surface props scatter; (5) Cursor pre-build gate; (6) cave layout+spline; (7) route floor/ceiling mesh+NavMesh; (8) shell rings+materials; (9) gameplay props+mobs; (10) validation bots (read-only, no full playtest ladder); (11) polish/post only if grade fails. AAA: Far Cry 5 — freshwater masks before cliffs before biomes; tools pass 2D masks between Houdini steps; nightly full regen only on build machines, not per artist click. Guerrilla HZD — GPU procedural placement after terrain rules; artist graph defines order; runtime placement is downstream of terrain theme. UE5 PCG — topological task order from graph edges; pin seeds while debugging; bake-time default, runtime only when streaming requires it. SideFX — heightfield LOD upsample chain; scatter points separate from terrain mesh; masks drive both erosion and scatter density. NMS/indie — single master seed + deterministic sub-seeds per cell; generate on demand from coordinates, do not store full world. Bot: read this entry + Packages/com.cursor.environment-authoring-kit/docs/WORLD-GENERATION-PIPELINE-LADDER.md before changing queue order; ContinueCaveGeometryAfterPreBuild must skip rungs 0–5.

visualReferences.title: Ubisoft — Far Cry 5 tool chain order (freshwater → cliffs → biomes)
visualReferences.notes: Sequential Houdini Engine tools exchange terrain masks: freshwater splines produce water surface + waterside assets + water mask; cliff tool consumes slope masks; biome tool consumes freshwater/road/fence/cliff masks. Sectors 64×64m minimum bake unit. Nightly world regen on build farm — editor sessions bake incrementally per sector. Kit analog: surface hydrology/trails before CavePrefabScatter.

visualReferences.title: Guerrilla — Horizon GPU runtime procedural placement (GDC 2017)
visualReferences.notes: Artist rule graph → GPU placement of full environment slices (audio, VFX, wildlife, gameplay) around player. Terrain theme and roads are inputs to placement rules, not regenerated by scatter. Kit analog: prop/scatter rungs after terrain+trail masks exist; do not rebake terrain when placing props.

visualReferences.title: Epic — UE5 PCG graph execution order and bake vs runtime
visualReferences.notes: Compiler weak-orders tasks: successors after predecessors; PreExecute/PostExecute bracket graph. Pin node seeds while debugging; world-position seeds in shipping. Prefer bake-time PCG; runtime PCG only for streaming cells. bIgnoreLandscapeTracking prevents accidental full regen on unrelated landscape edits — kit analog: scoped AssetDatabase refresh + phase completion flags.

visualReferences.title: SideFX — Heightfield creation → mask → scatter ladder
visualReferences.notes: Low-res heightfield → detail passes → upsample → re-apply erosion/distortion per LOD step. Keep scatter points separate from terrain geometry (Keep Incoming Terrain off on scatter). Masks from noise/erosion drive COPs texturing and scatter density. Kit analog: SurfaceTerrainAiPhases ladder + separate prop scatter after terrain grade passes.

visualReferences.title: Hello Games — No Man's Sky deterministic coordinate generation
visualReferences.notes: 64-bit master seed cascades to sub-seeds; planet content from coordinates + algorithms (Perlin, domain warp, biome rules) without storing worlds on servers. Positive/negative space passes for cliffs vs caves. Kit analog: CaveLayoutRoll + per-phase seed; session gate caches research/compile per seed — do not re-export 123 prompts on continue-after-pre-build.

visualReferences.title: NVIDIA Research — Fly, Fail, Fix (iterative level repair)
visualReferences.notes: Validation/repair loops run after generation, adjusting only failed subsystems from playtest traces — not full regen. Kit analog: validation substeps + SurfaceTrailWalkabilityRepair capped passes; never invoke full CavePlaytestPreBuildPipeline inside surface route probe.

visualReferences.title: 2018 USGS LiDAR DEM — Florida Panhandle (1 m bare-earth)
visualReferences.notes: Primary 1 m hydro-flattened DEM for NW panhandle. Use DAV to subset Bay/Washington/Jackson/Calhoun tiles; import heightmap into Unity Terrain for surface walk-in mouth alignment.

visualReferences.title: NOAA Coastal Inundation DEM — Florida Panhandle East
visualReferences.notes: Sea-level viewer DEM mosaic; includes Washington, Bay, and Calhoun. Good for coastal elevation context near Panama City / Chipley corridor.

visualReferences.title: Panama City FL Coastal DEM (Bay County — NCEI)
visualReferences.notes: Bay County / Panama City coastal bathy-topo DEM (~10 m grid). Use for entrance terrain scale near Gulf coast; combine with 2018 1 m lidar where overlapping.

visualReferences.title: Pensacola FL Coastal DEM (adjacent to Bay County)
visualReferences.notes: Western panhandle coastal DEM context (Pensacola). Useful when aligning cave mouth toward Gulf / low relief coastal plain.

visualReferences.title: NW Florida Water Management District — panhandle LiDAR (Jackson / Calhoun)
visualReferences.notes: Inland panhandle counties (Marianna / Blountstown / Chipley area) covered by 2018 panhandle lidar delivery. Contact NWFWMD for LAS; use NOAA bulk GeoTIFF for DEM hillshade.

visualReferences.title: USGS 3DEP / National Map — elevation (panhandle fallback)
visualReferences.notes: Fallback seamless DEM if county tile subset fails. Export 1 m or 1/3 arc-sec for Unity Terrain carve tests.

visualReferences.title: USGS DS 926 — Floridan aquifer structural surfaces & unit thickness (GIS rasters)
visualReferences.notes: Top/base of aquifer system and major hydrogeologic units — use thickness rasters and structural contours for cave void scale and ceiling depth. Skip 10,000 mg/L TDS (saline) boundary and any water-table symbology.

visualReferences.title: USGS PP 1807 — Revised Floridan aquifer hydrogeologic framework
visualReferences.notes: Companion report to DS 926: unit boundaries, faults, and framework plates. Map underground unit contacts to tube segmentation — not spring flow or heads.

visualReferences.title: USGS DS 584 — Floridan aquifer digital surfaces (legacy RASA)
visualReferences.notes: Scanned structural contours and unit extents. Fallback when DS 926 subset unavailable; still structure-only (no hydrology time series).

visualReferences.title: FGS / FDEP — Subsidence & karst incident reports (statewide GIS)
visualReferences.notes: Reported sinkholes and subsidence polygons — use as collapse pocket / choke point seeds. Filter to target counties; do not model standing water in depressions.

visualReferences.title: FDEP GeoData — karst-tagged layers
visualReferences.notes: DEP open karst/subsidence-related layers. Western panhandle has fewer exposed karst features (thick sediment) — combine with bare-earth LiDAR for entrance relief.

visualReferences.title: FGS OFMS 104 — LiDAR geomorphology (springs/sinkholes/caves on DEM)
visualReferences.notes: Methodology: LiDAR DEM + kriged top-of-rock for overburden thickness; plate 3 geomorphology with spring/sinkhole/cave locations. Apply workflow to panhandle tiles — map voids and collapse, not spring discharge volumes.

visualReferences.title: 2018 FL Panhandle LiDAR — Class 2 ground (cave mouth structure)
visualReferences.notes: Use classified ground (class 2) LAS/DEM for surface walk-in and ceiling height reference. Ignore class 9 water, hydro breaklines, and inundation products for underground tube layout.

visualReferences.title: USGS 3DEP — bare-earth elevation (aquifer mouth alignment)
visualReferences.notes: National seamless DEM fallback. Subset per county bbox; hillshade PNGs under ResearchCache/images/fl-{county}-hillshade/.

visualReferences.title: NWFWMD — GIS / hydrogeology data portal (panhandle districts)
visualReferences.notes: District aquifer and geology layers for Jackson/Calhoun/Washington corridors. Prefer unit boundaries and potentiometric structure maps over surface-water features.

visualReferences.title: USGS Caribbean-Florida Water Science Center — Floridan aquifer publications
visualReferences.notes: Regional studies on framework and karst; use cross-sections and structure maps for tube gradient, not spring monitoring hydrographs.

visualReferences.title: Florida statewide LiDAR assessment (FGIO / DEP)
visualReferences.notes: Index for county LAS/DEM coverage; links to 2018 panhandle delivery and LABINS. Structure-only: DEM/LAZ for rock relief, not bathymetric surfaces.

floridaTerrain.policy: Cave structure only: bare-earth LiDAR (class 2), Floridan aquifer structural surfaces (USGS DS 926), karst/subsidence polygons. Do not use water table, TDS, bathymetry, inundation DEMs, or spring discharge for underground void layout.
